assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map (impact-map.md) explicitly identifies 5 ambiguities: (1) 'No Performance Targets for "Faster" Search', (2) 'No Definition of "More Relevant" Results', (3) 'No Specification of Which Filters to Add', (4) 'No Quantified Non-Functional Requirements', (5) 'No Existing Performance Baseline'. All are clearly labeled as 'Ambiguity 1' through 'Ambiguity 5' and relate directly to vagueness in the feature description.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map contains a 'Scope Exclusions' section stating: '"Better UI" (Non-MVP) — Excluded. The "Better UI" requirement is marked as non-MVP and cannot be planned: No frontend repository is available in the project configuration; No design mockups or UI specifications have been provided; Search UI improvements require frontend work that is out of scope for trustify-backend. This requirement is excluded entirely from the implementation plan.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All four task files (task-1 through task-4) follow the template structure with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies. Optional sections like API Changes, Reuse Candidates, and Verification Commands are included where applicable and omitted where not, per the template rules.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks contain explicitly labeled assumptions. Task 1: 'Assumption pending clarification: The specific fields to index for each entity are assumed...' and 'Assumption pending clarification: The text search configuration is assumed to be english.' Task 2: 'Assumption pending clarification: The relevance ranking weights are assumed to use default PostgreSQL ts_rank weights.' and 'Assumption pending clarification: It is unclear whether search should return a unified result list...' Task 3: 'Assumption pending clarification: The feature description says "Some kind of filtering capability"...' and 'Assumption pending clarification: The filter parameter format is assumed to be a query parameter type=sbom,advisory...' Task 4 does not contain assumptions, but that is reasonable as it is a test task that depends on the assumptions already documented in Tasks 1-3.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All four task files contain '## Repository' followed by 'trustify-backend' as a single repository name. Task 1 line 1-2: '## Repository\ntrustify-backend'. Task 2 line 1-2: same. Task 3 line 1-2: same. Task 4 line 1-2: same.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain all required sections. Task 1: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 31), '## Test Requirements' (line 39). Task 2: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 43), '## Test Requirements' (line 51). Task 3: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 39), '## Test Requirements' (line 48). Task 4: '## Target Branch' (line 4), '## Description' (line 7), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 33), '## Test Requirements' (line 41).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All file paths referenced in Files to Modify/Create match the repo-backend.md structure. Task 1 creates 'migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs' (follows pattern of existing migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs) and modifies 'migration/src/lib.rs' (exists in manifest). Task 2 modifies 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs' — all present in repo-backend.md. Task 3 modifies the same search and common files. Task 4 modifies 'tests/api/search.rs' — present in repo-backend.md. The new migration file m0002 follows the established naming pattern from m0001_initial and is not an invented path but a logical new file following existing conventions.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs' as the pattern to follow. Task 2 references 'common/src/db/query.rs' with specific helper function descriptions, 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs' with SearchService details, 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs' with endpoint contract details, 'common/src/model/paginated.rs' for PaginatedResults<T>, and 'common/src/error.rs::AppError'. Task 3 references 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', and 'common/src/db/query.rs' with specific implementation details about query parameter handling and filter combinators. Task 4 references 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', and 'tests/api/advisory.rs' with specific test patterns like 'assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)'.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks have '## Target Branch\nmain'. Task 1 lines 4-5, Task 2 lines 4-5, Task 3 lines 4-5, Task 4 lines 4-5.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks end with a description digest line. Task 1: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:82c28ab53ddc88ebd5af1bfc6f402d881d3a6f5984dd982b52a49f4912cb5f12' (64 hex chars). Task 2: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:0c8a5e73770b6f95581ae83370360034ff39d6ee63033512d2c930c42f8be383' (64 hex chars). Task 3: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:a60fb995d9c3af32aebe2c92ad1ed654a4bc315c378dbc5a361502f78d8aeda2' (64 hex chars). Task 4: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:1a0179d021efbf0abf76ac70f9ea2594f6e1f57f308f1212382eac5b488de29d' (64 hex chars). All use the 'sha256-md:' prefix followed by exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Note: we cannot verify these were computed by re-fetching from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py from output files alone, but the format is correct.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: Convention enrichment follows the prescribed format across all tasks. Task 1: 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §Module pattern: follow the established migration directory structure. Applies: task creates `migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs` matching the convention's migration file scope.' Task 2 includes three convention references all with correct format: '§Error handling...Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's `.rs` endpoint file scope.', '§Response types...Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's endpoint file scope.', '§Query helpers...Applies: task modifies `common/src/db/query.rs` matching the convention's query helper scope.' Task 3 includes two: '§Error handling' and '§Query helpers' with matching file references. Task 4: '§Testing...Applies: task modifies `tests/api/search.rs` matching the convention's test file scope.' No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. All rationales follow the prescribed format, not free-form prose.
